metadata.title: High School — Algebra
domains: Arithmetic with Polynomials and Rational Expressions
metadata.notes: Auto-ingested from Common Standards Project (set 67810E9EF6944F9383DCC602A3484C23_D10003FB_high-school-algebra). Node structure matches KnowledgeNode in knowledge_zome/integrity/src/lib.rs.

nodes.title: Graph the solutions to a linear inequality in two variables as a half-plane (...
nodes.description: Graph the solutions to a linear inequality in two variables as a half-plane (excluding the boundary in the case of a strict inequality), and graph the solution set to a system of linear inequalities in two variables as the intersection of the corresponding half-planes.

academic_standards.description: Graph the solutions to a linear inequality in two variables as a half-plane (excluding the boundary in the case of a strict inequality), and graph the solution set to a system of linear inequalities in two variables as the intersection of the corresponding half-planes.

nodes.title: Explain why the x-coordinates of the points where the graphs of the equations...
nodes.description: Explain why the x-coordinates of the points where the graphs of the equations y = f(x) and y = g(x) intersect are the solutions of the equation f(x) = g(x); find the solutions approximately, e.g., using technology to graph the functions, make tables of values, or find successive approximations. Include cases where f(x) and/or g(x) are linear, polynomial, rational, absolute value, exponential, and logarithmic functions.

academic_standards.description: Explain why the x-coordinates of the points where the graphs of the equations y = f(x) and y = g(x) intersect are the solutions of the equation f(x) = g(x); find the solutions approximately, e.g., using technology to graph the functions, make tables of values, or find successive approximations. Include cases where f(x) and/or g(x) are linear, polynomial, rational, absolute value, exponential, and logarithmic functions.

nodes.title: Understand that the graph of an equation in two variables is the set of all i...
nodes.description: Understand that the graph of an equation in two variables is the set of all its solutions plotted in the coordinate plane, often forming a curve (which could be a line).

academic_standards.description: Understand that the graph of an equation in two variables is the set of all its solutions plotted in the coordinate plane, often forming a curve (which could be a line).

nodes.title: (+) Find the inverse of a matrix if it exists and use it to solve systems of ...
nodes.description: (+) Find the inverse of a matrix if it exists and use it to solve systems of linear equations (using technology for matrices of dimension 3 × 3 or greater).

academic_standards.description: (+) Find the inverse of a matrix if it exists and use it to solve systems of linear equations (using technology for matrices of dimension 3 × 3 or greater).

nodes.title: (+) Represent a system of linear equations as a single matrix equation in a v...
nodes.description: (+) Represent a system of linear equations as a single matrix equation in a vector variable.

academic_standards.description: (+) Represent a system of linear equations as a single matrix equation in a vector variable.

nodes.title: Solve a simple system consisting of a linear equation and a quadratic equatio...
nodes.description: Solve a simple system consisting of a linear equation and a quadratic equation in two variables algebraically and graphically.

academic_standards.description: Solve a simple system consisting of a linear equation and a quadratic equation in two variables algebraically and graphically.

nodes.title: Solve systems of linear equations exactly and approximately (e
nodes.description: Solve systems of linear equations exactly and approximately (e.g., with graphs), focusing on pairs of linear equations in two variables.

academic_standards.description: Solve systems of linear equations exactly and approximately (e.g., with graphs), focusing on pairs of linear equations in two variables.

nodes.title: Prove that, given a system of two equations in two variables, replacing one e...
nodes.description: Prove that, given a system of two equations in two variables, replacing one equation by the sum of that equation and a multiple of the other produces a system with the same solutions.

academic_standards.description: Prove that, given a system of two equations in two variables, replacing one equation by the sum of that equation and a multiple of the other produces a system with the same solutions.

nodes.title: Solve quadratic equations in one variable
nodes.description: Solve quadratic equations in one variable.

academic_standards.description: Solve quadratic equations in one variable.

nodes.title: Solve linear equations and inequalities in one variable, including equations ...
nodes.description: Solve linear equations and inequalities in one variable, including equations with coefficients represented by letters.

academic_standards.description: Solve linear equations and inequalities in one variable, including equations with coefficients represented by letters.

nodes.title: Solve simple rational and radical equations in one variable, and give example...
nodes.description: Solve simple rational and radical equations in one variable, and give examples showing how extraneous solutions may arise.

academic_standards.description: Solve simple rational and radical equations in one variable, and give examples showing how extraneous solutions may arise.

nodes.title: Explain each step in solving a simple equation as following from the equality...
nodes.description: Explain each step in solving a simple equation as following from the equality of numbers asserted at the previous step, starting from the assumption that the original equation has a solution. Construct a viable argument to justify a solution method.

academic_standards.description: Explain each step in solving a simple equation as following from the equality of numbers asserted at the previous step, starting from the assumption that the original equation has a solution. Construct a viable argument to justify a solution method.

nodes.title: Rearrange formulas to highlight a quantity of interest, using the same reason...
nodes.description: Rearrange formulas to highlight a quantity of interest, using the same reasoning as in solving equations.

academic_standards.description: Rearrange formulas to highlight a quantity of interest, using the same reasoning as in solving equations.

nodes.title: Represent constraints by equations or inequalities, and by systems of equatio...
nodes.description: Represent constraints by equations or inequalities, and by systems of equations and/or inequalities, and interpret solutions as viable or nonviable options in a modeling context.

academic_standards.description: Represent constraints by equations or inequalities, and by systems of equations and/or inequalities, and interpret solutions as viable or nonviable options in a modeling context.

nodes.title: Create equations in two or more variables to represent relationships between ...
nodes.description: Create equations in two or more variables to represent relationships between quantities; graph equations on coordinate axes with labels and scales.

academic_standards.description: Create equations in two or more variables to represent relationships between quantities; graph equations on coordinate axes with labels and scales.

nodes.title: Create equations and inequalities in one variable and use them to solve problems
nodes.description: Create equations and inequalities in one variable and use them to solve problems. Include equations arising from linear and quadratic functions, and simple rational and exponential functions.

academic_standards.description: Create equations and inequalities in one variable and use them to solve problems. Include equations arising from linear and quadratic functions, and simple rational and exponential functions.

nodes.title: (+) Understand that rational expressions form a system analogous to the ratio...
nodes.description: (+) Understand that rational expressions form a system analogous to the rational numbers, closed under addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division by a nonzero rational expression; add, subtract, multiply, and divide rational expressions.
nodes.subdomain: Arithmetic with Polynomials and Rational Expressions

academic_standards.description: (+) Understand that rational expressions form a system analogous to the rational numbers, closed under addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division by a nonzero rational expression; add, subtract, multiply, and divide rational expressions.

nodes.title: Rewrite simple rational expressions in different forms
nodes.description: Rewrite simple rational expressions in different forms; write <sup>a(x </sup>/<sub>b(x)</sub> in the form q(x) + <sup>r(x)</sup>/<sub>b(x)</sub>, where a(x), b(x), q(x), and r(x) are polynomials with the degree of r(x) less than the degree of b(x), using inspection, long division, or, for the more complicated examples, a computer algebra system.
nodes.subdomain: Arithmetic with Polynomials and Rational Expressions

academic_standards.description: Rewrite simple rational expressions in different forms; write <sup>a(x </sup>/<sub>b(x)</sub> in the form q(x) + <sup>r(x)</sup>/<sub>b(x)</sub>, where a(x), b(x), q(x), and r(x) are polynomials with the degree of r(x) less than the degree of b(x), using inspection, long division, or, for the more complicated examples, a computer algebra system.

nodes.title: (+) Know and apply the Binomial Theorem for the expansion of (x + y)<sup>n</s...
nodes.description: (+) Know and apply the Binomial Theorem for the expansion of (x + y)<sup>n</sup> in powers of x and y for a positive integer n, where x and y are any numbers, with coefficients determined for example by Pascal's Triangle.
nodes.subdomain: Arithmetic with Polynomials and Rational Expressions

academic_standards.description: (+) Know and apply the Binomial Theorem for the expansion of (x + y)<sup>n</sup> in powers of x and y for a positive integer n, where x and y are any numbers, with coefficients determined for example by Pascal's Triangle.

nodes.title: Prove polynomial identities and use them to describe numerical relationships
nodes.description: Prove polynomial identities and use them to describe numerical relationships.
nodes.subdomain: Arithmetic with Polynomials and Rational Expressions

academic_standards.description: Prove polynomial identities and use them to describe numerical relationships.

nodes.title: Identify zeros of polynomials when suitable factorizations are available, and...
nodes.description: Identify zeros of polynomials when suitable factorizations are available, and use the zeros to construct a rough graph of the function defined by the polynomial.
nodes.subdomain: Arithmetic with Polynomials and Rational Expressions

academic_standards.description: Identify zeros of polynomials when suitable factorizations are available, and use the zeros to construct a rough graph of the function defined by the polynomial.

nodes.title: Know and apply the Remainder Theorem: For a polynomial p(x) and a number a, t...
nodes.description: Know and apply the Remainder Theorem: For a polynomial p(x) and a number a, the remainder on division by x - a is p(a), so p(a) = 0 if and only if (x - a) is a factor of p(x).
nodes.subdomain: Arithmetic with Polynomials and Rational Expressions

academic_standards.description: Know and apply the Remainder Theorem: For a polynomial p(x) and a number a, the remainder on division by x - a is p(a), so p(a) = 0 if and only if (x - a) is a factor of p(x).

nodes.title: Understand that polynomials form a system analogous to the integers, namely, ...
nodes.description: Understand that polynomials form a system analogous to the integers, namely, they are closed under the operations of addition, subtraction, and multiplication; add, subtract, and multiply polynomials.
nodes.subdomain: Arithmetic with Polynomials and Rational Expressions

academic_standards.description: Understand that polynomials form a system analogous to the integers, namely, they are closed under the operations of addition, subtraction, and multiplication; add, subtract, and multiply polynomials.

nodes.title: Derive the formula for the sum of a finite geometric series (when the common ...
nodes.description: Derive the formula for the sum of a finite geometric series (when the common ratio is not 1), and use the formula to solve problems.

academic_standards.description: Derive the formula for the sum of a finite geometric series (when the common ratio is not 1), and use the formula to solve problems.

nodes.title: Choose and produce an equivalent form of an expression to reveal and explain ...
nodes.description: Choose and produce an equivalent form of an expression to reveal and explain properties of the quantity represented by the expression.

academic_standards.description: Choose and produce an equivalent form of an expression to reveal and explain properties of the quantity represented by the expression.

nodes.title: Use the structure of an expression to identify ways to rewrite it
nodes.description: Use the structure of an expression to identify ways to rewrite it.

academic_standards.description: Use the structure of an expression to identify ways to rewrite it.
